title: horkelia fusca subsp. parviflora
intro: Horkelia fusca subsp. parviflora, also known as small-flowered horkelia, is a perennial flowering plant native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which can help to soothe and heal wounds, reduce swelling, and prevent infection. The health benefits of Horkelia fusca subsp. parviflora include its ability to support the immune system, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, making it a popular herbal remedy for skin conditions and minor injuries. Herbal preparations of Horkelia fusca subsp. parviflora can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used topically or taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
